lede: A look at February 2 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Valéry Giscard d'Estaing and James Joyce, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Slovenia
events.desc: Slovenian NBA player Luka Doncic is traded from the Dallas Mavericks to the Los Angeles Lakers in exchange for Anthony Davis in one of the largest trades in American sports history.

events.title: MV Rabaul Queen
events.desc: The ferry MV Rabaul Queen sinks off the coast of Papua New Guinea near the Finschhafen District, with an estimated 146–165 dead.

events.title: Export–Import Bank of the United States
events.desc: The Export-Import Bank of the United States is incorporated.

events.title: Grand Central Terminal
events.desc: Grand Central Terminal opens in New York City.

events.title: Mexican–American War
events.desc: Mexican–American War: The Treaty of Guadalupe Hidalgo is signed.

events.title: Johann Sebastian Bach
events.desc: J. S. Bach leads the first performance of his chorale cantata Mit Fried und Freud ich fahr dahin, BWV 125, based on Luther's paraphrase of the Nunc dimittis.

events.title: Alexander Selkirk
events.desc: Alexander Selkirk is rescued after being shipwrecked on a desert island, inspiring Daniel Defoe's adventure book Robinson Crusoe.

events.title: Transylvanian peasant revolt
events.desc: Nine leaders of the Transylvanian peasant revolt are executed at Torda.

events.title: Terra Mariana
events.desc: Terra Mariana, eventually comprising present-day Latvia and Estonia, is established.

events.title: Alaric II
events.desc: Alaric II, eighth king of the Visigoths, promulgates the Breviary of Alaric (Breviarium Alaricianum or Lex Romana Visigothorum), a collection of "Roman law".

births.name: Valéry Giscard d'Estaing
births.line: French academic and politician, 20th President of France

births.name: James Joyce
births.line: Irish novelist, short story writer, and poet

deaths.text: Sid Vicious, English singer and bass player
Natalie Clifford Barney, American author, poet, and playwright
Bertrand Russell, English mathematician and philosopher, Nobel Prize laureate
Carl Friedrich Goerdeler, German economist and politician
Giovanni Pierluigi da Palestrina, Italian composer and educator
